title: Python Execution, Tools, and Evidence · Spiral Review
summary: Build and diagnose a small command-line learning log across REPL, script, editor, and terminal workflows. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a reproducible command-line learning log after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain how source, the interpreter, objects, and standard streams participate when a Python program runs.
Choose REPL, script, module, or notebook execution based on the task and reproducibility needs.
Read syntax errors and tracebacks from the final exception back to the relevant source cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
